content:
- Executed plan for etcd watch bootstrap timeout: in src/dcs/etcd_store.rs, connect_with_worker_bootstrap_timeout no longer blocks on worker join when startup recv_timeout hits Timeout; it sends Shutdown, drops command_tx, and detaches the JoinHandle. Also distinguishes RecvTimeoutError::Disconnected with a clearer error.
- Strengthened timeout regression test by injecting a 2.5s establish delay + asserting connect returns <1s, with an outer 2s timeout so join-regressions fail deterministically.
- Added RealEtcdFixture::restart_preserve and a new reconnect test that verifies a non-empty snapshot after restart produces Reset + leader PUT and rebuilds cache authoritatively.
- Added store-level ordering regression test for Put(stale), Reset, Put(fresh).
- Validation: make check OK; make test initially hit Makefile 120s timeout on cold compile, rerun warm passed; make test-long flaked once (transport error submitting /switchover) then passed on rerun; make lint OK.
- Updated task file with checkmarks and passes=true, ran .ralph/task_switch.sh, committed + pushed. Appended AGENTS.md with learning about intermittent test-long /switchover transport flake and pushed follow-up commit.

- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md and enforced bug-first rule.
- Found two bug tasks with <passes>false</passes>: BDD HTTP tests false-pass + HA e2e false-pass.
- Chose next task: .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-ha-e2e-false-pass-via-best-effort-polling-and-timestamp-fallback.md (highest impact: HA e2e can silently pass under transport/polling errors and timestamp=0 fallback undermines fencing assertions).
- Wrote that path to .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Inspected src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs around wait_for_all_failsafe (1412-1458), wait_for_all_nodes_failsafe (1460+), and scenario call sites at ~2099-2100 and ~2484-2500.
- Confirmed semantic mismatch: wait_for_all_failsafe returns on (!has_primary && observed_failsafe_nodes non-empty), but matrix scenario log says "fail-safe observed on all nodes". Timeout/error text in wait_for_all_failsafe also incorrectly claims "all nodes observed".
- Noted strict path already exists (wait_for_all_nodes_failsafe + dedicated strict scenario) and matrix currently uses non-strict helper. Also noted strict helper itself does not require every node to report FailSafe, only all nodes observed non-primary + at least one FailSafe.
- Plan recommendation: keep matrix best-effort semantics and fix wording/assertions for honesty; keep strict-all-nodes guarantees in dedicated strict scenario to avoid extra flake/timeout risk in matrix path.
- Next if asked to implement: rename helpers/logs for explicit semantics, adjust matrix log text, optionally add an explicit post-wait no-primary best-effort assert in matrix, run targeted e2e tests then full make test gate.

- Read prior progress log and inspected `src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs` around the user-cited lines.
- Confirmed bug pattern: in `run_sql_workload_worker`, successful commit timestamp capture uses `match unix_now()` with `Err(_) => 0`; later no-quorum fencing computes `commits_after_cutoff` via `timestamp > cutoff_ms`, so fallback zeros are excluded and can undercount post-cutoff commits.
- Verified `ha_e2e::util::unix_now()` already returns `Result<UnixMillis, WorkerError>`; swallowing happens at call sites.
- Traced workload aggregation (`stop_sql_workload_and_collect`) and saw it preserves `committed_writes` while collecting raw timestamps, so missing timestamps are currently invisible to assertions.
- Checked test layout: this file currently has only heavy `#[tokio::test]` real-e2e tests, no focused unit tests for cutoff counting / timestamp validity.
- Opened and applied add-bug skill, created `.ralph/tasks/bugs/fencing-cutoff-commit-timestamp-zero-fallback-undercounts.md` to track fix with acceptance criteria.
- Next step if continuing: implement fail-closed timestamp handling (no sentinel 0), add helper for cutoff evaluation that errors on incomplete/invalid timestamp data, then add focused unit tests plus update no-quorum e2e assertion path.

- Investigated src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs around assert_no_dual_primary_window and all in-file callers.
- Confirmed concrete false-pass bug: assert_no_dual_primary_window ignores cluster_ha_states() errors and returns Ok at deadline even if every poll failed; also each poll can block up to E2E_HTTP_STEP_TIMEOUT (20s) so a 3-6s window may complete with zero valid observations and still pass.
- Traced callers at lines ~1895/1964/2070/2090/2225/2379: these use the function as primary split-brain guard, so false-pass weakens scenario guarantees.
- Noted adjacent related weakness: assert_no_dual_primary_in_samples only checks max_concurrent_primaries and can false-pass when sample_count == 0 (all poll errors).
- Also inspected partition chaos analog: its assert_no_dual_primary_window uses best-effort partial state and can pass with unreachable nodes; likely needs explicit coverage policy if unified.
- Next step: report deterministic fail-closed criteria, suggested signature/behavior changes, and ripple effects/tests/harness tweaks without code edits unless user requests implementation.

- Read .ralph/current_task.txt -> .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-ha-e2e-false-pass-via-best-effort-polling-and-timestamp-fallback.md and reviewed the task description.
- Spawned parallel subagents to inspect: (a) assert_no_dual_primary_window false-pass + 20s per-poll timeout issue, (b) wait_for_all_failsafe semantic/log mismatch, (c) unix_now() -> 0 commit timestamp fallback weakening fencing cutoff assertion, and (d) repo-wide scan showing similar patterns in e2e_partition_chaos.rs and shared harness.
- Manually inspected src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs around the referenced line ranges and confirmed:
  - assert_no_dual_primary_window ignores polling errors and can return Ok with 0 successful samples; also can block up to E2E_HTTP_STEP_TIMEOUT so a 3-6s window may contain no evidence.
  - wait_for_all_failsafe returns on "no primary + any FailSafe" but messages/logs claim "all nodes".
  - SQL workload commit timestamps use unix_now() with Err => 0 sentinel, which undercounts post-cutoff commits in the no-quorum fencing test.
- Wrote a detailed, step-by-step plan into .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-ha-e2e-false-pass-via-best-effort-polling-and-timestamp-fallback.md and marked it with TO BE VERIFIED per workflow.

- Next (required by workflow): do a DEEP + SKEPTICAL verification of the plan (must change at least one plan item), then replace TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE before implementing; after execution, run make check/test/test-long/lint.

- Re-inspected current src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs around wait_for_all_failsafe/wait_for_all_nodes_failsafe and refreshed exact line references.
- Confirmed semantic mismatch remains: wait_for_all_failsafe succeeds on (no primary + at least one FailSafe) but timeout text and matrix scenario log claim all nodes observed.
- Traced call sites: matrix test calls wait_for_all_failsafe directly and logs "fail-safe observed on all nodes"; strict helper path uses wait_for_all_nodes_failsafe and logs all-nodes text.
- Noted subtle naming gap in strict helper too: wait_for_all_nodes_failsafe requires all nodes observed non-primary + at least one FailSafe, not every node FailSafe.
- Also confirmed stop_etcd_majority_and_wait_failsafe (best-effort wrapper) is currently unused; matrix bypasses it.
- Next step: provide user with explicit semantics-vs-name map and recommend minimal refactor plan (best-effort rename/log-text cleanup, preserve strict scenario as dedicated stronger check).

- Inspected src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs for timestamp collection and fencing-cutoff assertions.
- Confirmed fallback-to-0 at ClusterFixture::record (line ~315) and run_sql_workload_worker commit timestamp capture (line ~1718).
- Confirmed no-quorum fencing assertion counts commits_after_cutoff by filtering raw committed_at_unix_ms > cutoff (line ~2620), so sentinel 0 undercounts and can false-pass.
- Also confirmed adjacent fail-open behavior: assert_no_dual_primary_window ignores cluster_ha_states() errors and can pass with zero valid samples; assert_no_dual_primary_in_samples does not require sample_count > 0.
- Identified additional sentinel defaults in StressScenarioSummary::failed started/finished timestamps set to 0; low risk for enforcement but harms artifact semantics.
- Next step if continuing in code: introduce explicit timestamp validity tracking (Option or invalid counter), fail-closed cutoff evaluator helper with invariants, tighten no-dual-primary assertions to require successful observations, and add unit tests for timestamp/cutoff + observation fail-closed behavior without real binaries.

- Re-inspected src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs around ClusterFixture::assert_no_dual_primary_window and helper chain cluster_ha_states -> poll_node_ha_states_best_effort_with_timeout -> util::get_ha_state_with_fallback.
- Verified exact false-pass paths: (1) assert_no_dual_primary_window swallows cluster_ha_states errors and returns Ok at deadline even when every poll failed; (2) poll can block far beyond window because per-node timeout is effectively up to ~4x http_step_timeout via primary timeout + fallback connect/write/read each timed; with E2E_HTTP_STEP_TIMEOUT=20s a single node can stretch one poll toward ~80s while loop still returns Ok if no dual primary seen.
- Noted related sampled-helper weakness: assert_no_dual_primary_in_samples passes when sample_count==0, so strict no-dual assertions on sampled windows can also false-pass under total API outage.
- Tricky runtime interaction: polling uses spawn_local on current-thread runtime and awaits all joins; adding outer tokio::timeout around cluster_ha_states would drop JoinHandles and detach tasks, risking task buildup and extra flake.
- Proposed minimal fail-closed changes: require >=1 successful full-cluster observation before assert_no_dual_primary_window can pass (surface last poll error if none), and require sample_count>0 in assert_no_dual_primary_in_samples; add unit tests around pure decision helpers so no real binaries/network needed.
